page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance scored 1864.49, while multi-core reached 15504.4. Memory bandwidth tests showed read-only small block speeds of 849.57 GB/sec, dropping to 102.07 GB/sec for large blocks, with write speeds at 59.04 GB/sec. In database operations, the instance achieved a PassMark database score of 7565.93 and processed 15.46 million Redis SET operations per second.

For machine learning workloads, the instance demonstrates strong text generation performance with a small 135M LLM model, reaching 254.84 tokens/sec. However, performance drops significantly on larger models, with medium 7B model prompt processing falling into the weak tier at 30.95 tokens/sec. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.r7.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for in-memory databases like Redis, caching layers, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores (16 vCPUs) powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, but includes no local storage or GPU resources. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a PassMark database score of 7565.93 and 15.46 million Redis SET operations per second. While it exhibits strong text generation speeds of 254.84 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models, performance degrades on larger models. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU resources without local storage needs.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized KVM instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
